methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Thunder Tiger operates in the Defense & Security sector as a public company, having raised $22M+ as of 2025 under CEO Gene Su. Valuation analysis draws from a broader universe of 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable. While 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, filtering for stage-aligned, valuation-tier-matched comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. Narrow-universe peer analysis risks compounding selection bias; accordingly, an all-sector median would lack explanatory power specific to Thunder Tiger's market positioning and capital efficiency.

The absence of a sufficient matched peer set means traditional comparable-company analysis cannot anchor a valuation range. Alternative approaches—revenue multiples, DCF-based methods, or transaction comps within Defense & Security—would require operational and financial data not provided here. The current dataset supports only the observation that Thunder Tiger competes within a sparse comparable universe, with measurable peers concentrated in adjacent subsectors. Further valuation work requires either (1) operational metrics (revenue, growth trajectory, unit economics) or (2) transaction precedent in the narrow Defense & Security frontier-tech band. Without such inputs, a quantitative range cannot be responsibly synthesized.
